title: austroderia turbaria
intro: Austroderia turbaria, also known as rough tussock, is a type of grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Austroderia turbaria include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and soothing irritated throats. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
